Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Look up the current user's identity (Javier Morales) using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Javier" or "Javier Morales") to establish the persona's email (javier.morales@moveops.com) and role (Relocation Coordinator).
OE 2: Search Airtable relocations using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to find all relocation records where the Assigned Coordinator is "Javier Morales." This should return active/in-progress moves including: Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy Health, Philadelphia → Denver), Diego Castaneda (PivotPoint Labs, Brooklyn → Austin), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic Robotics, Columbus → Seattle), Priya Suresh (Vectral Systems, Portland → Denver), Joaquín Reyes (GreenStack Energy, Phoenix → Seattle), Rachel Dominguez (Canopy Health, Los Angeles → Portland), Sasha Greenwald (PivotPoint Labs, New York → Nashville), Jordan Whitfield (BrightLoop Analytics, Minneapolis → Denver). It should also return completed moves (Kevin Tran, Lisa Petrosky, Sasha Petrov, Marcus Rivera, Ravi Sundaram, Ben Schaefer, and one completed Danielle Osei record) as well as the cancelled Danielle Osei move (rec_reloc_osei, Status: Cancelled).
OE 3: Search Linear for issues assigned to the current user using linear_list_issues (assignee: "moveops_javier_morales" or query: "Javier"). Discovers Danielle Osei's tickets are still active: #310 (Apartment Search, state_in_progress), #311 (Flight Booking, state_todo), #312 (Moving Coordination, state_todo) - all assigned to Javier despite the Airtable relocation record showing Danielle Osei's move as "Cancelled."
OE 4: Search emails for the DOT compliance audit using search_emails (query: "DOT compliance" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat audit" or "placarding"). Finds: (a) Marcus Thorne's email forwarding the PHMSA inquiry from Linda Castellano to Chloe, (b) Chloe's email to Marcus identifying 4 hazmat moves including Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics as having a critical documentation gap - the signed DOT placarding certificate was never filed, (c) Chloe's email to Greg Pallone at Swift requesting the retroactive certificate, (d) Greg Pallone's reply confirming Swift driver Mike Becerra completed the placarding and providing the signed certificate. This reveals that the DOT documentation gap flagged by Chloe is for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics relocation (Epilog Fusion Pro 48 industrial laser cutter with Class 3B CO2 laser tube) - the signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate was never filed internally by MoveOps, although Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations has confirmed the placarding was completed at time of transport.
OE 5: Search Slack #operations channel using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "DOT" or "hazmat" or "compliance" or "Keiko Tanaka" or "storage" or "Tyler") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C006") to find internal discussions about the compliance audit, coordinator assignments, and relocation logistics.
OE 6: Search CRM for deals related to Javier's moves using crm_search_deals or crm_get_deal, and retrieve engagement notes using crm_list_engagements. Finds the Tyler Okonkwo deal (deal_canopy_okonkwo_apr2026, $2,160, storage quote pending), the Diego Castaneda deal (deal_pivot_april_2026_dual, $10,650 for Okonjo + Castaneda, crating inspection noted), the Keiko Tanaka deal (deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026, $2,770), and CRM engagement notes confirming the storage surcharge and crating inspection details.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks for the Canopy Health invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: matching "Canopy" or "INV-2026-0401") or quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: the invoice ID). Finds invoice INV-2026-0401 totaling $9,210 with a $600 temporary storage fee for Tyler Okonkwo marked as pending client approval - confirms the invoice is blocked waiting on Javier's storage facility quote.
OE 8: Search the calendar for events around April 10-11 using calendar_search_events (query: "crating" or "Diego" or "inspection") or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (date range covering April 10-11, 2026). The calendar is empty - no events are scheduled. This confirms that Diego Castaneda's pre-move crating inspection has NOT been scheduled with the movers yet, which is a gap to report.
OE 9 : Post a message in the #operations Slack channel (C006) via conversations_add_message about the DOT documentation gap. The message should identify the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics move as the one with the missing DOT hazmat placarding certificate, and describe what action is needed (e.g., confirming the signed certificate from Swift Relocations has been obtained and filed, or escalating to Chloe if not).
OE 10 : Send an email to chloe.vance@moveops.com via send_email from javier.morales@moveops.com regarding the DOT compliance documentation gap. The email should identify the Keiko Tanaka move as the one with the gap, mention the missing placarding certificate, and describe what needs to happen to resolve it.
OE 11 : Update Danielle Osei's stale Linear tickets via linear_update_issue to a closed or cancelled state. There are 3 tickets: #310 (Apartment Search, currently state_in_progress), #311 (Flight Booking, currently state_todo), #312 (Moving Coordination, currently state_todo). The Airtable relocation record shows the Danielle Osei move as "Cancelled," so these tickets should no longer be active.
OE 12 : The final response identifies Keiko Tanaka's relocation (Mosaic Robotics, Columbus → Seattle) as the one with the DOT compliance documentation gap (the DOT hazmat placarding certificate for the Epilog Fusion Pro laser cutter was never filed internally, though Swift Relocations has confirmed the placarding was completed at time of transport).
OE 13 : The final response reports that Tyler Okonkwo's Denver temporary storage facility quote is still pending from Javier and that the Canopy Health invoice (INV-2026-0401, $9,210 total, with $600 storage surcharge) cannot be finalized until the quote is submitted.
OE 14 : The final response reports on Diego Castaneda's pre-move crating inspection for the Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill ($42,000 insured value), noting the employee requested the inspection for April 10 or 11, 2026 at his Brooklyn apartment (312 Wythe Ave, Unit 4B). The agent should indicate whether the inspection has been scheduled with the assigned moving vendor (Heartland Movers per CRM deal record).
OE 15 : The final response flags Danielle Osei's Vectral Systems relocation as cancelled in Airtable while her Linear tickets (#310, #311, #312) were still showing as active - and confirms they were cleaned up.
OE 16 : The final response provides a comprehensive overview of all of Javier's active in-progress assignments with gaps or overdue items, listing at least 5 of: Tyler Okonkwo, Priya Suresh, Joaquín Reyes, Diego Castaneda, Keiko Tanaka, Rachel Dominguez, Sasha Greenwald, Jordan Whitfield.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
